standard: Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act — FTC Safeguards Rule (16 CFR Part 314) — 2023 Amended Requirements
description: Mapping of GLBA Safeguards Rule technical requirements to AWS Config managed rules and GuardDuty finding types. Covers financial institutions' obligations to protect customer financial information (NPI — Nonpublic Personal Information).

controls.control_name: Access Controls — Limit Access to Customer Information
controls.description: Implement access controls that limit access to customer information only to authorized users with a legitimate business need. Controls must prevent unauthorized access to NPI.

controls.control_name: Access Controls — MFA for Remote Access
controls.description: Implement multi-factor authentication for any individual accessing any information system containing customer financial information, unless the covered entity uses equivalent compensating controls.
Encrypt all customer information held or transmitted by the financial institution, both in transit over external networks and at rest.
All customer NPI transmitted over external networks must be encrypted. TLS must be enforced on all endpoints handling NPI.
Adopt procedures for audit logging to monitor the activity of authorized users and detect unauthorized access to or use of customer information.
Implement continuous monitoring to detect actual and attempted attacks on or intrusions into information systems containing customer NPI.

controls.control_name: Network Security — Segregation and Flow Logging
controls.description: Implement controls to detect changes to information and information system activity, including monitoring network traffic to identify anomalous activity.
Customer NPI must never be publicly accessible via S3 or any storage service. Public access must be blocked at the bucket and account level.
Customer information stored in S3 must be protected against unauthorized modification. Public write access must be blocked.
Implement secure development practices including controls to ensure secure API endpoints. Lambda functions serving customer NPI must not be publicly accessible.
All requests to S3 buckets containing customer information must use TLS. Non-TLS requests must be denied by bucket policy.
Implement controls for the secure management of credentials used to access systems holding customer NPI, including automatic rotation of secrets.
Define and periodically implement a written incident response plan. Security events affecting NPI must be detected, logged, and responded to in a defined timeframe.
